why: The sovereignty block is REQUIRED and closed: exactly the five sub-fields, no more, no fewer (schemas/envelope.schema.json properties.sovereignty). Baseline accept.
model_class is in the block's required list; an envelope missing any of the five sub-fields fails validation (src/envelope.ts:156-178).
additionalProperties:false on the block — an unknown sub-field is rejected, not ignored (schema line 74).
data_residency pattern is ^[A-Z]{2}$ — uppercase only (schema line 51-54).

FINDING: the ^[A-Z]{2}$ pattern admits codes ISO 3166-1 leaves unassigned (ZZ, XX) and the informal EU convention. Shape-valid but semantically meaningless — the surface a sender uses to evade residency gating (OD-4).

MASKING: frontier_ok=false + model_class=frontier is schema-valid (independent fields, no cross-field constraint) yet unsatisfiable — no cloud model may process it but only frontier models are permitted. No path decides the combination (OD-1).

parseSovereignty (src/envelope.ts:614-629) is myelin's ONLY reader of frontier_ok/model_class: canReachFrontier = frontier_ok && model_class!=='local-only'. It is advisory — no myelin enforcement path calls it (OD-1).
Baseline: a local envelope to an in-allowlist local.* subject passes the classification budget and allowed_subjects (engine.ts + egress.ts).
CLASSIFICATION_PREFIX_BUDGET: local may target only local.* — a local envelope cannot publish to a federated.* subject (egress.ts). Holds even with block_local_escape off.
block_local_escape fast-path (engine.ts:99-106): a local envelope whose target does not begin with local. is blocked before rule evaluation; the reason string carries 'block_local_escape'.

COLLISION: the downward-superset budget makes public->local.* a DELIBERATE allow (egress.ts CLASSIFICATION_PREFIX_BUDGET), while namespace.md's strict prefix<->classification alignment calls the same pair a protocol violation. The two definitions contradict (OD-3).

MASKING: residency gating works when the code IS a key in data_residency_constraints and the subject matches — this correct path masks the fail-open in the paired vector egress/residency-unlisted-fail-open.
A CH-constrained envelope targeting a federated.de.* subject not matching the CH pattern list is blocked (checkDataResidency, egress.ts).

FINDING (fail-open): a residency code absent from data_residency_constraints is unconstrained — 'if (!constraints) return ALLOW' (egress.ts:60-70). Declaring ZZ (an unassigned code the pattern admits) evades residency gating entirely. A fix under OD-4 deletes/flips this vector.
Fail-closed on unsigned: no signed_by identity => unknown-principal, INDEPENDENT of reject_unknown_partners (ingress.ts). An unsigned envelope can never satisfy ingress.
Baseline federated crossing: last-stamp identity in imported_principals, subject inside local_scope, requirements subset of max_capabilities (ingress.ts checkScopeCeiling).
A mapped partner whose source subject falls outside its local_scope is scope-exceeded (checkScopeCeiling, ingress.ts).
A requirements[] tag outside max_capabilities is scope-exceeded — the capability ceiling constrains a MAPPED partner (ingress.ts).

FINDING (trust inversion): with reject_unknown_partners=false, an UNMAPPED principal gets an unconditional ALLOW that skips both the local_scope check and the capability ceiling, while the mapped partner in the same policy is constrained. Declaring a partner reduces its access relative to a stranger's (ingress.ts:63-74). A fix under OD-5 flips this vector.
With reject_unknown_partners=true an unmapped principal is unknown-principal — the fail-closed posture the operator guide's minimum policy ships (sovereignty-operator.md 2).

FINDING: the F-5 enforcement channel's own nak default source (transport.ts:38) has 2 segments, but the envelope source grammar (RFC-0003) requires exactly 3 — {principal}.{stack}.{assistant}. The nak ships via the raw transport (bypassing validateEnvelope), so any subscriber that schema-validates inbound rejects the very message explaining the block (OD-6).
